description:
This diagram illustrates the two main phases of a satellite's polar orbit: ascending and descending.
The left panel depicts a satellite in an "Ascending orbit", shown moving from the South (S) pole towards the North (N) pole. The red arrow along the dashed red orbital path indicates this upward trajectory. A yellow arrow emanating from the satellite points towards a region of Africa, illustrating the direction of data acquisition or sensing during this pass.
The right panel depicts the same satellite concept in a "Descending orbit", shown moving from the North (N) pole towards the South (S) pole. The red arrow along the dashed red orbital path indicates this downward trajectory. A yellow arrow emanating from the satellite points towards a region spanning from Europe to North Africa, illustrating the direction of data acquisition or sensing during this pass.
Both Earth illustrations show visible landmasses including Africa, Europe, and parts of Asia, emphasizing the ground track relative to the continents.
